text:
Dwelling units are classified in Group R. A detached accessory dwelling unit
serving a one- or two-family dwelling is regulated under the California
Residential Code; where an ADU is within or attached to a larger Group R building,
the applicable Group R occupancy and fire-separation provisions of this code apply.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Building code (CBC) · CBC §310 — Residential Occupancy Classification

section: CBC §705 — Exterior Walls and Fire Separation Distance
text:
The fire-resistance rating of exterior walls and the allowable area of openings
are determined by the fire separation distance to the property line or to an
assumed imaginary line between buildings on the same lot. Walls with a fire
separation distance of less than 5 feet require a one-hour fire-resistance rating
and limited openings.
citation: CBC §705 — Exterior Walls and Fire Separation Distance
context: Los Angeles, CA · Building code (CBC) · CBC §705 — Exterior Walls and Fire Separation Distance

text:
Stairways serving an occupied space shall have a minimum width, maximum riser
height of 7 3/4 inches, minimum tread depth of 10 inches, and handrails as
required. Refer to the Residential Code for stairways within dwelling units.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Building code (CBC) · CBC §1011 — Stairways

text:
A detached accessory dwelling unit shall not exceed 1,200 square feet of floor
area. An ADU of up to 800 square feet is permitted by right on any lot regardless
of floor-area-ratio, lot coverage, or open-space standards. Floor area is measured
to the interior face of the exterior walls.
context: Los Angeles, CA · City / municipal code · LAMC §12.22 A.33(c) — Unit Size

text:
A detached accessory dwelling unit shall provide a side yard setback of not less
than 4 feet. No setback is required for a legally existing permitted structure
that is converted to an ADU, or for a new ADU built in the same footprint.
context: Los Angeles, CA · City / municipal code · LAMC §12.22 A.33(d) — Side Setback

text:
A detached accessory dwelling unit shall provide a rear yard setback of not less
than 4 feet measured from the property line to the nearest wall of the unit.
context: Los Angeles, CA · City / municipal code · LAMC §12.22 A.33(d) — Rear Setback

text:
One off-street parking space is required per accessory dwelling unit. This
requirement is waived where the ADU is within one-half mile walking distance of
public transit, within an architecturally and historically significant district,
or where on-street permit parking is required but not offered to the ADU.
context: Los Angeles, CA · City / municipal code · LAMC §12.22 A.33(e) — Parking

section: LA City Planning — ADU Submittal Requirements
text:
A complete ADU application must include a plot plan drawn to scale, floor plans,
building elevations for all sides, and a Title-24 energy compliance report.
Applications missing any required sheet are deemed incomplete and are not routed
for plan check.
citation: LA City Planning — ADU Submittal Requirements
context: Los Angeles, CA · City / municipal code · LA City Planning — ADU Submittal Requirements

section: LA County Code §22.140.640 — ADU Development Standards
text:
In unincorporated Los Angeles County, a detached accessory dwelling unit shall
not exceed 1,200 square feet and shall maintain side and rear setbacks of not less
than 4 feet. ADUs are permitted in any zone that allows a single-family or
multifamily dwelling.
citation: LA County Code §22.140.640 — ADU Development Standards
context: Los Angeles, CA · County code · LA County Code §22.140.640 — ADU Development Standards

text:
A detached accessory dwelling unit in unincorporated County territory is limited
to 16 feet, increased to 18 feet within one-half mile of a major transit stop,
consistent with state law.
context: Los Angeles, CA · County code · LA County Code §22.140.640 — Height

section: LA County Code Title 26 — Building Code Adoption
text:
The County adopts the California Building Standards Code (Title 24) with local
amendments. Construction of an accessory dwelling unit must comply with the
adopted building, residential, plumbing, mechanical, electrical, and green
building standards as amended by the County.
citation: LA County Code Title 26 — Building Code Adoption
context: Los Angeles, CA · County code · LA County Code Title 26 — Building Code Adoption

text:
Plumbing fixtures and fittings shall meet the maximum flow rates: water closets
1.28 gallons per flush, showerheads 1.8 gallons per minute at 80 psi, residential
lavatory faucets 1.2 gallons per minute, and kitchen faucets 1.8 gallons per
minute. These limits achieve the required reduction in indoor water use.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Green building standards (CALGreen) · CALGreen §4.303.1 — Water Conserving Plumbing Fixtures

text:
Automatic irrigation system controllers installed at the time of final inspection
shall be weather-based or soil-moisture-based and comply with the Model Water
Efficient Landscape Ordinance.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Green building standards (CALGreen) · CALGreen §4.304.1 — Outdoor Water Use / Irrigation

text:
New one- and two-family dwellings and townhouses with attached private garages
shall provide EV-ready circuits. The service panel shall be sized and a raceway
installed to accommodate a dedicated 208/240-volt branch circuit for future
electric vehicle supply equipment.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Green building standards (CALGreen) · CALGreen §4.106.4 — Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging

text:
A minimum of 65 percent of the nonhazardous construction and demolition waste
generated at the site shall be diverted from landfill by recycling or reuse, with
documentation provided to the enforcing agency.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Green building standards (CALGreen) · CALGreen §4.408.1 — Construction Waste Reduction

section: CPC §401 — Minimum Plumbing Fixtures per Dwelling Unit
text:
Each dwelling unit, including an accessory dwelling unit, shall be provided with a
kitchen sink, a water closet, a lavatory, and a bathtub or shower. Each required
fixture shall be connected to an approved water supply and drainage system.
citation: CPC §401 — Minimum Plumbing Fixtures per Dwelling Unit
context: Los Angeles, CA · Plumbing code (CPC) · CPC §401 — Minimum Plumbing Fixtures per Dwelling Unit

text:
Each dwelling unit shall be served by a water heater of adequate capacity.
Storage water heaters shall be anchored or strapped at the upper and lower
one-third of the appliance to resist seismic displacement, and shall discharge
through an approved temperature-and-pressure relief valve to an approved location.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Plumbing code (CPC) · CPC §501 — Water Heater Requirements

text:
The number and type of plumbing fixtures shall be based on the occupancy and
number of occupants. Fixtures shall be spaced to provide the clearances required
for use and cleaning.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Plumbing code (CPC) · CPC §422 — Fixture Count and Accessibility

text:
Habitable space, hallways, and portions of basements containing these spaces shall
have a ceiling height of not less than 7 feet. Bathrooms, toilet rooms, and
laundry rooms shall have a ceiling height of not less than 6 feet 8 inches.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Residential code (CRC) · CRC R305 — Minimum Ceiling Height

section: CRC R310 — Emergency Escape and Rescue Openings
text:
Basements and every sleeping room shall have at least one operable emergency
escape and rescue opening with a minimum net clear opening of 5.7 square feet, a
minimum net clear height of 24 inches, and a minimum net clear width of 20 inches.
citation: CRC R310 — Emergency Escape and Rescue Openings
context: Los Angeles, CA · Residential code (CRC) · CRC R310 — Emergency Escape and Rescue Openings

text:
Smoke alarms shall be installed in each sleeping room, outside each separate
sleeping area in the immediate vicinity of the bedrooms, and on each story. Alarms
shall be interconnected and hardwired with battery backup in new construction.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Residential code (CRC) · CRC R314 — Smoke Alarms

text:
Carbon monoxide alarms shall be provided outside each separate sleeping area in
the immediate vicinity of the bedrooms in dwelling units that have a fuel-burning
appliance or an attached garage.
context: Los Angeles, CA · Residential code (CRC) · CRC R315 — Carbon Monoxide Alarms

text:
A detached accessory dwelling unit may be up to 16 feet in height. The limit
increases to 18 feet where the parcel is within one-half mile walking distance of
a major transit stop or high-quality transit corridor, or supports an existing or
proposed multistory multifamily dwelling, with an additional two feet permitted to
align the roof pitch with the primary dwelling. A local agency may not impose a
height limit below this state floor.
context: Los Angeles, CA · State code · CA Gov. Code §66321 / HCD — Height (Detached)

text:
An attached accessory dwelling unit may rise to 25 feet or the height limit that
applies to the primary dwelling under the local zoning code, whichever is lower.
An ADU is not required to be shorter than 16 feet.
context: Los Angeles, CA · State code · CA Gov. Code §66321 / HCD — Height (Attached)

text:
No setback greater than 4 feet from the side and rear lot lines may be required
for an accessory dwelling unit that is not a conversion of existing space. No
setback may be required for an existing permitted structure or one constructed in
the same footprint.
context: Los Angeles, CA · State code · CA Gov. Code §66323 / HCD — Setbacks

section: CA Gov. Code §66317 / HCD — Ministerial Approval
text:
A permit application for an accessory dwelling unit shall be considered and
approved ministerially without discretionary review within 60 days of a complete
application. Failure to act within 60 days deems the application approved.
citation: CA Gov. Code §66317 / HCD — Ministerial Approval
context: Los Angeles, CA · State code · CA Gov. Code §66317 / HCD — Ministerial Approval
